mechanism_narrative: Higher trailing 3-month growth in retail sales signals firm household demand and near-term revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate without a simultaneous unemployment spike signals policy easing rather than recession stress, lowering discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.25pp over 3 months and UNRATE rises by no more than 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have lower average 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered observations or if the positive hit rate is below 52% in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive trailing 3-month growth in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month growth is not associated with higher subsequent 60-day SPX returns than negative INDPRO growth, or if the spread is statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and reduced construction-linked spending, leading investors to price slower earnings growth and negative SPX returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 15% versus 12 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations or if triggered returns exceed the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates combined with falling retail sales indicate monetary tightening is suppressing demand, reducing expected corporate revenues and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months and RSAFS falls over 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with demand deterioration
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either single condition alone, or if triggered returns are positive in more than 55% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices raises expected inflation compensation and the probability of tighter policy, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth by more than 1pp
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below the unconditional 5-day average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a non-rising unemployment rate signals broad labor-market expansion without immediate slack deterioration, supporting income growth and positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE 3-month change is less than or equal to 0pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor expansion trigger fails to outperform the unconditional 5-day SPX return by at least 0.25 percentage points or if its positive hit rate is below 52%.
